note: Provenance for upstream work Jaimitos actually adopted or materially consulted. Pinned by SHA. There is no automatic updater by design — see integrations/README.md for the manual update process.

deviations: REJECTED the SessionStart bootstrap hook, `using-superpowers`, `brainstorming`, `writing-plans`, `subagent-driven-development`, `executing-plans`, `finishing-a-development-branch` and `dispatching-parallel-agents`: each is a competing spine (router / planner / executor / completion gate / swarm). Jaimitos already has exactly one of each, and importing one drags the cross-referenced chain in with it.

REJECTED `requesting-code-review` as a skill: v2.7.0 deliberately retired Jaimitos' own review skills in favour of Claude Code's native /code-review and /security-review, plus scope-guard and the independent evaluator.

`verification-before-completion` was MERGED into existing surfaces (executor, evaluator, /phase, /wrap) rather than added as a skill — a verification skill would become a second, weaker completion gate alongside scripts/tick.sh.

`test-driven-development`'s absolute 'delete the code and start over' rule was softened to Jaimitos' explicit-exception model: production code that must precede the red test is allowed, but the exception has to be recorded. The no-meaningful-red-means-not-TDD rule is kept.

`systematic-debugging`'s 4-phase structure was NOT imported (Jaimitos' `diagnose` already has a stronger 6-phase loop). Only the missing mechanisms were merged: the evidence taxonomy, the one-hypothesis rule, and the 3-fix architecture escalation.
`using-git-worktrees` is REFERENCE ONLY — Jaimitos already has worktree flows and this release adds no worktree framework.
Voice: upstream's 'your human partner' is rewritten to Jaimitos' 'the user' throughout.

REJECTED `improve-codebase-architecture`: it renders a Tailwind/Mermaid HTML report app, which this release explicitly rules out. Its *friction signals* (shallow modules, pass-through layers, leaky seams, the deletion test) were merged into `mapme`, which already owns docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. No separate architecture-audit capability was added.

REJECTED `code-review` as a skill (see the v2.7.0 decision above), but ADOPTED its central idea: the Standards-vs-Spec split is exactly the two-axis evaluator this release introduces. A condensed Fowler smell baseline feeds the evaluator's engineering-quality axis.
`codebase-design` was adapted to `module-design`; its DESIGN-IT-TWICE.md was NOT ported because Jaimitos already merged that as the `design-twice` skill in v2.5.0.

`domain-modeling` was MERGED into the existing `glossary` skill rather than added: docs/GLOSSARY.md must stay the single glossary authority and docs/decisions/ the single ADR location. Matt's CONTEXT.md / docs/adr/ layout is not used.

`prototype`'s LOGIC.md and UI.md were collapsed inline to avoid extra context cost, and its evidence semantics were tightened for this scaffold: prototype output may serve an explicitly scoped prototype/research phase but may never satisfy production implementation or release criteria.

All Matt skills are tracker-centric upstream (GitHub Issues, CONTEXT.md, a work-queue subsystem). Every adaptation is docs-centric (docs/SPEC.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, docs/decisions/, docs/GLOSSARY.md) with zero tracker dependency — consistent with the v2.5.0 adaptations.

Upstream has since DEPRECATED `design-an-interface` and `ubiquitous-language`, folding them into `codebase-design` and `domain-modeling` respectively — independent confirmation of the merge decisions taken here.
